human_actions_pending.context: 7-section paper with conditional theorem, ready for Overleaf

decisions.decision: Collision time is t=-g0^2/8 (not g0^2/4 — Session 15 had factor-of-2 error)
decisions.rationale: Verified numerically: 2-body ODE dg/dt=+4/g gives g^2=g0^2+8t, collision at -g0^2/8
The 1.88 was from the wrong formula. Neighbors slow collision by ~5-10%

decisions.decision: Log-concavity is necessary but NOT sufficient for RH
decisions.rationale: Complex zeros off the critical line can be hidden — their contribution to log|xi| on the real line doesn't create sign changes

decisions.decision: The proof barrier is identified: proving f''_zeta < 0 without Hadamard product is equivalent to RH
decisions.rationale: The Hadamard product proof of log-concavity assumes RH (all gamma_n real). Non-circular proof requires controlling zeta on the critical line from the Euler product side

decisions.decision: Conditional theorem: Montgomery -> Lambda squeezed to 0 from below
decisions.rationale: GUE gap statistics give g_min ~ N^{-1/3}, collision time ~ N^{-2/3}/log^2(N) -> 0

next_action: Either: (1) Push Jensen computation to higher precision to get better sigma^2 estimate and extend Turan verification past n=20, or (2) Start new direction entirely — the dBN path has been thoroughly mapped and the barrier identified. The r>0 bridge variance identity from Sessions 11-14 or the spectral operator approach are alternative angles.
context_notes: Session 16 was an analytical deep-dive into the dBN framework. Started by scaling the N-body ODE to 200 zeros, then systematically explored three proof paths: (1) collision time formula (clean, 5% GUE correction), (2) Burgers/log-concavity (barrier identified — circular), (3) Lambda convergence (conditional theorem under Montgomery). Found and corrected a factor-of-2 error from Session 15. Wrote a full preprint and connected to GORZ Jensen polynomials. The sigma^2 ~ 1.37 GORZ width parameter is the quantitative bridge between the algebraic and dynamical approaches. Environment: /c/Python313/python.exe, matplotlib Agg backend.
